name: user-blocking-question
description: Agent's reply ends in a question mark, signalling it's waiting for user input. Validates the trailing-? path through the state classifier (session.IsWaitingForUserInput → state_classifier.go decision 2a). The recipe deliberately stops at the waiting episode and does not answer the question — locking down the entry into waiting. The coalesced-flush second-scenario variant (#150) is covered by unit tests around ShouldSynthesizeCollapsedWaiting.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

## Scenario

The canonical primitive THIS cell exercises is `tool_calls` + AskUserQuestion (or adapter equivalent): the agent pauses mid-turn to ask the user a question via a **dedicated structured question tool call**, the dashboard enters `waiting` and stays there until the user replies. User-observable signal: `ready → working → waiting`, then after the reply `waiting → working → ready`, with the `waiting` episode visible. The load-bearing word is *structured tool call* — this cell maps the AskUserQuestion-tool primitive, NOT the natural-language trailing-`?` text path (that path is the sibling cell `agent-question-pending`, assessed yes/full/ready). The scenario's own recipe preconditions make the split explicit: "aider has NO structured AskUserQuestion tool — the user-blocking-question/aider coverage cell is the verdict for that STRUCTURED-tool primitive and does NOT describe [the trailing-? text] cell."

## agent_supports: partial

Aider has **no structured AskUserQuestion-equivalent tool call**. A scan of aider 0.86.2's source confirms the only question surfaces are:

- `Commands.cmd_ask` (`/ask`) — a *chat-mode switch* (ask the LLM about the code without it editing files), not a tool the model invokes to gate on the user.
- `InputOutput.confirm_ask` / `prompt_ask` — interactive y/n / free-text prompts at the TUI layer (file-add confirmations, URL offers), not a model-driven structured question.

The capabilities profile agrees: the Primitive-exercised anchor key `tool_calls` is `true` (aider does call Edit/Bash tools), but `structured_output_jsonl: false` and there is no dedicated question-tool primitive. So the *dedicated structured question tool* in the scenario's primitive does not exist — hence `partial` rather than `yes`. What aider DOES do behaviorally (end a turn with natural-language question prose and stop) is the essence of the user-blocking pause, but it is expressed as prose, not a structured tool call — which is exactly why the verdict is `partial` and why the observable path it would trip belongs to the sibling text-classifier cell, not this structured-tool cell.

## daemon_capability: incapable

For the STRUCTURED-tool primitive this cell maps, the behavior leaves **no trace** in the watched Source. Aider is `FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}` — a markdown transcript, not JSONL. The parser (`parser.go`) can emit exactly: `user_message` (`#### `), `assistant_message` (per `> Tokens:` model-call line), `tool_result` synthesized ONLY for Edit (`> Applied edit to`) and Bash (`> Running`), and `turn_done` (LLM-error blockquote or 1500ms IdleFlush). There is no branch that can emit a structured question event, and — decisively — aider writes no AskUserQuestion marker into the markdown for the parser to key on. No tool-open/tool-result pair brackets a question; the `toolCall` synthesizer fires only on edit/shell blockquotes. With no trace in the Source, no parser change could surface this primitive's structured `working → waiting → working` tool-gate — so the verdict is `incapable` (architecture: accept), not `bug` (which would require a trace the daemon mis-handles).

This is honest, not defensive: the recipe's working→waiting CAN be observed via the trailing-`?` text classifier (`IsWaitingForUserInput` on `LastAssistantText`, state_classifier decision 2a, pinned by `TestParser_TrailingQuestionMark_PreservedForWaitingClassification`) — but that is the SIBLING cell's primitive. Mapping it onto THIS structured-tool cell would mis-credit a different mechanism. For the AskUserQuestion-tool primitive specifically, the daemon is `incapable`.

## driver_capability: ready

The interactive driver (`scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh`) implements every step the recipe needs — `send`, `wait_turn`, `sleep`. No gapped primitive (restart/reset/resume/sigkill/keys/fork) is required. The recording is deferred only for an out-of-band reason (no provider auth in the onboarding environment), which is not a driver-grammar gap. So `driver_capability: ready`.

## Matrix

The coverage cell already reads `partial` / `daemon_capability: incapable` / `driver_capability: ready` — this re-assessment ratifies it. The drift is only in this artifact, which still carried the old single-axis `irrlicht_observes: yes` (a claim that borrowed the sibling text-path mechanism). Rewriting to the two-axis schema removes that conflation: for the structured-tool primitive, `daemon_capability: incapable`.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: the user-blocking pause is real and would be observable as a natural-language question, but aider expresses it as trailing-? prose, NOT a structured AskUserQuestion tool call — there is no tool-open/tool-result pair around the question for the parser to key on. The observable trailing-? path is the SIBLING cell agent-question-pending (yes/full/ready), not this structured-tool cell.

Recording is deferred for an out-of-band reason: in the onboarding environment aider has no provider auth (headless aider hangs ~5min on the OpenRouter OAuth flow). This is not a driver-grammar gap — driver_capability stays ready.

Were a recording attempted via the trailing-? prose path, the working→waiting transition lands on the synthesized turn_done (IdleFlush ~1500ms, clamped to the ~5s working-refresh tick), not instantaneously — but that path measures the sibling cell's primitive, not this one's structured tool gate.

sources.note: FilesUnderCWD markdown RawLineParser; emits user_message/assistant_message/tool_result(Edit,Bash)/turn_done only — no branch emits a structured question event, and no AskUserQuestion marker is written to the markdown for it to key on. Grounds daemon_capability:incapable (no trace in Source).
Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}; no PendingContributor/SubagentCounter — confirms the markdown transport carries no structured tool-call channel.
Primitive anchor tool_calls:true (Edit/Bash) but structured_output_jsonl:false and no dedicated question-tool primitive — grounds agent_supports:partial for the AskUserQuestion-tool primitive.
cmd_ask (/ask) is a chat-mode switch, not a model-invoked structured question tool (aider 0.86.2).
confirm_ask/prompt_ask are TUI y/n prompts, not a model-driven structured question; ai_output->append_chat_history writes assistant reply prose (incl. any question) into .aider.chat.history.md.
Aider docs: /ask, /code, /architect are chat modes — confirms no dedicated structured AskUserQuestion tool.

TestParser_TrailingQuestionMark_PreservedForWaitingClassification pins the trailing-? TEXT path — the mechanism of the SIBLING cell agent-question-pending, NOT this structured-tool cell; cited to show the observable path is text-classifier, not a tool gate.

preconditions: SCOPE: aider has NO structured AskUserQuestion tool — the user-blocking-question/aider coverage cell (partial/no) is the verdict for that STRUCTURED-tool primitive and does NOT describe this cell. This cell records the adapter-agnostic trailing-? TEXT-classifier path: the assistant ends its reply with a literal '?', and irrlicht enters waiting via session.IsWaitingForUserInput (state_classifier.go decision 2a), not via a tool marker. agent_supports:partial (no structured tool); irrlicht_observes:yes (text path).

aider CLI on PATH and authenticated against a configured model (aider runs against its provider out-of-band; this cell needs a live model to elicit a real clarifying-question turn). NOTE: in the onboarding environment aider has no provider auth (headless aider hangs ~5min on the OpenRouter OAuth flow), so recording is deferred — the spec is the documented target until auth is available.
tmux available — the interactive driver runs aider in a tmux REPL so the session stays alive past turn end and the working→waiting episode is observable
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: The interactive driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh) git-inits a fresh per-run cwd, launches `aider --no-auto-commits --yes-always --no-gitignore` in a tmux session, and waits for the repo-map banner line in .aider.chat.history.md before sending the prompt

aider is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} — there is no transcript-UUID rollout session; the whole arc lives on the one proc-<PID> session the daemon's process scanner keys (cf. basic-turn). The transcript materialises lazily on first input, but the proc-<PID> pre-session is observable from launch

Unlike the headless `aider --message` path (single-shot, the process exits at turn end so the working→waiting episode is lost to a process_exited race — see the basic-turn recipe), the interactive driver keeps the REPL ALIVE after the turn; it is torn down only at script end via `tmux kill-session` (no Ctrl-D / /exit), so the daemon can observe the post-turn waiting dwell

No reply is sent to the question — after wait_turn + a trailing sleep the session dwells in waiting, then the driver kills the tmux session once, so aider exits and the daemon emits a single process_exited while the session is still waiting

script.text: I want to add a feature to my project but I'm not sure which file it should go in. Ask me exactly ONE short clarifying question to scope it, ending in a question mark, and then stop and wait for my answer. Output only that one question and nothing else.

verify: events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for the proc-<PID> session within ~2s of REPL launch (a transient pre-session may also appear; the turn arc lands on the bindable session)
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the session
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→working on the session when the prompt is sent
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→waiting on the SAME session when the turn ends (the reply ends in a question mark; the classifier takes the trailing-? text path rather than →ready)

the session's final live state is waiting and it persists ≥4s — no state_transition to ready appears before the REPL is torn down (the working→waiting settle lands a few seconds late via the ~1500ms IdleFlush clamped to the ~5s working-refresh tick; the 8s trailing sleep covers that latency)
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited on the session at REPL teardown while it is still waiting (one long-lived session, not a per-turn exit)

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code ships the `AskUserQuestion` tool, which is the agent-side mechanism the spec is asking about. When the assistant emits a tool_use block with `name: "AskUserQuestion"`, the TUI pauses until the user answers. The parser at `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/parser.go` records this in two ways: (1) `collectToolUse` appends `{Name: "AskUserQuestion"}` to `ev.ToolUses`, and (2) the prompt text is surfaced as `ev.AssistantText` (the question becomes the user-facing waiting header).

irrlicht observes the spec's two variants via the state classifier (`core/application/services/state_classifier.go`):

**Variant 1 — question opens, user replies later.** While `AskUserQuestion` is the open tool, `metrics.NeedsUserAttention()` is true (the user-blocking tool list in `core/pkg/tailer/tailer_config.go` covers AskUserQuestion + ExitPlanMode + question). Rule 1 of `ClassifyState` transitions `working → waiting`. Belt-and-braces, the PreToolUse hook (`hooks.go` toolAskUserQuestion branch) calls `HandlePermissionHook` and flips `PermissionPending=true`, which independently fires rule 0 (`permission prompt open → waiting`) — so even if the JSONL tool_use line is delayed by the buffered TUI writer (#307), the waiting episode is still recorded. When the user replies, the next user message clears the open tool and a fresh assistant message drives `waiting → working → ready`.

**Variant 2 — coalesced flush.** When fswatcher batches the tool_use, tool_result, and the next assistant text into one tailer pass, the open-tool window closes inside that pass. The tailer sets `SawUserBlockingToolClosedThisPass=true`, and `ShouldSynthesizeCollapsedWaiting` (state_classifier.go:126) emits a synthetic `working → waiting` followed by `waiting → working → ready` so the user still sees the waiting episode (issue #150). The session does not stick in waiting because the closing event resolves `HasOpenToolCall=false` and `LastEventType=user` clears the user-blocking gate.
caveats: The PreToolUse hook only fires if the user has run the irrlicht hook installer (`hookinstaller.go`) into their Claude settings.json. Without the hook, irrlicht still catches the AskUserQuestion via the JSONL tool_use line — but with one fswatcher tick of latency. The recipe should not depend on whether the hook is installed; both paths converge on the same observable state arc.

The PreToolUse-only path can race the JSONL tool_use append on very small AskUserQuestion calls; the synthetic-waiting collapse (#150) plus `PermissionPending` flag are the two seatbelts that keep the waiting episode visible in all timing regimes.

sources.note: collectToolUse / scanMessageContent — AskUserQuestion tool_use is recorded in ev.ToolUses and its prompt becomes ev.AssistantText.
user-blocking tool list (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, question) — drives NeedsUserAttention().
ClassifyState rule 1 (user-blocking tool open → waiting) and ShouldSynthesizeCollapsedWaiting (#150 collapsed-flush synthesis).
PreToolUse handler for AskUserQuestion/ExitPlanMode flips PermissionPending (issue #307) so waiting fires even before the JSONL line lands.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID (run-cell.sh does this via uuidgen | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)

script.text: Reply with exactly one short clarifying question, ending in a question mark. No other text.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send
events.jsonl contains exactly one state_transition to working for the UUID
events.jsonl contains exactly one state_transition to waiting for the UUID after the assistant's question-mark reply
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to ready for the UUID after the waiting transition (recipe terminates while session is in waiting)
final session state is waiting
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 1 user line and 1 assistant line, with the assistant text ending in '?'

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Scope

This cell is the FULL user-blocking-question arc: the agent ends a turn with a clarifying question (working→waiting), the USER then answers, and the agent finishes the turn (waiting→working→ready), all on the same live rollout session. It extends the sibling `agent-question-pending` cell (same coverage_id `user-blocking-question`), which proved only the ENTRY into waiting (working→waiting) and deliberately stopped there. The scenario-meanings.md user-observable signal is explicit: `ready → working → waiting`; after the user replies, `waiting → working → ready`; the waiting episode is visible (no skip-over).

Note: codex has NO structured AskUserQuestion tool (matrix prior). This cell, like its sibling, exercises the adapter-agnostic trailing-`?` text path through the state classifier (session.IsWaitingForUserInput → state_classifier.go decision 2a), not a dedicated question tool. The scenario.json verify block names this path directly.

## agent_supports: yes

codex can be prompted to end its turn with a single clarifying question (literal `?`). It records the reply as a `response_item`/`message`(assistant) whose `output_text` carries the question, followed by an `event_msg`/`task_complete` turn-finish marker — confirmed against the sibling's recorded rollout. A subsequent user answer is just another turn on the same rollout session: codex resumes working and completes normally, ending the final turn in a non-question terminal reply (→ ready). This is standard multi-turn behaviour already proven by the multi-turn-conversation recording.

## daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready (interactive TUI, not headless)

The codex adapter is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.codex/sessions, JSONLineParser}`. The parser maps the assistant message to `assistant_message` (sets `AssistantText`) and `event_msg`/`task_complete` to `turn_done`. At the first turn's `task_complete`, `IsAgentDone()` is true AND `LastAssistantText` ends in `?`, so the classifier takes decision 2a (waiting) rather than 2b (ready). When the user sends the answer, the session goes working again; the final turn ends in a plain reply with no trailing `?`, so the classifier takes 2b (ready). The waiting→working→ready recovery is therefore observable on the SAME session_id.

The load-bearing constraint (inherited from the sibling) is session liveness: headless `codex exec` is single-shot and exits at turn end (process_exited race, see basic-turn spec), so the multi-turn arc would be lost. The interactive driver (drive-codex-interactive.sh, `codex --no-alt-screen` under tmux) keeps the rollout session ALIVE across both turns; the REPL is torn down only at script end. A trailing sleep after each wait_turn lets each state episode settle.

## Recipe implication

INTERACTIVE `script`: send a prompt that makes codex ask one clarifying question → wait_turn → sleep (settle into waiting) → send the ANSWER → wait_turn → sleep (settle into ready). The first reply must end in `?`; the final reply must NOT, so the terminal state is ready.
caveats: Observable ONLY via the interactive TUI — headless `codex exec` exits at turn end and both the waiting episode and the recovery would be lost to a process_exited race (basic-turn spec documents this). The recipe must be interactive.
Distinct from the structured AskUserQuestion path: codex has no question tool in its schema (matrix prior), so this is the trailing-`?` text classifier (decision 2a), which is adapter-agnostic.

The first reply must end in a literal `?` (→ waiting) and the FINAL reply after the user's answer must NOT end in `?` (→ ready). Steer the answer prompt so codex confirms/acts and ends on a statement, otherwise the terminal turn would re-enter waiting instead of ready.
Extends the sibling agent-question-pending (same coverage_id) which locked down only working→waiting; this cell adds the waiting→working→ready recovery after the user answers.

sources.note: sibling cell, same coverage_id user-blocking-question: yes/yes for the trailing-? entry into waiting; this cell extends it with the post-answer recovery
user-blocking-question user-observable signal: ready→working→waiting, then after user reply waiting→working→ready; waiting episode visible (no skip-over)
decision 2a (IsWaitingForUserInput → waiting) checked before 2b (IsAgentDone → ready)
IsWaitingForUserInput via ExtractQuestionSnippet (trailing ?); a non-question terminal reply drops to 2b (ready)
assistant_message sets AssistantText; event_msg/task_complete → turn_done; FilesUnderRoot watch
proves the interactive codex driver produces real per-turn working↔ready arcs on a single live rollout session — the basis for the waiting→working→ready recovery here
interactive driver keeps the REPL alive across turns (kill-session only at script end); supports send/wait_turn/sleep — the steps this recipe needs

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated (~/.codex/config.toml or OPENAI_API_KEY)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: The interactive driver launches `codex --no-alt-screen` in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/, accepts the trust dialog, and waits for the TUI banner + MCP boot before sending the first prompt

Unlike the headless `codex exec` path (single-shot, ends in process_exited at turn end — see the basic-turn recipe), the interactive driver keeps the rollout session ALIVE across BOTH turns; the REPL is torn down only when the script ends, so the daemon can observe the full waiting→working→ready recovery on one live session

script.text: I want to rename a function in my project, but I haven't told you which one. Before doing anything, ask me exactly one short clarifying question — which file and which function — ending in a question mark. Output ONLY that one question and nothing else; do not start editing.
Rename the function `oldName` to `newName` in src/util.js. Just describe in one or two sentences what you would change — do NOT ask any further questions and end on a plain statement, not a question.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created (proc-<PID>) event in ready within 1000 ms of agent launch
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the rollout session with a distinct session_id, born ready
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the rollout session_id
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→working on the rollout session_id when the first prompt is sent
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→waiting on the SAME rollout session_id when the first turn ends (the reply ends in a question mark; classifier takes the trailing-? path rather than →ready)
the waiting episode is visible and persists ≥3s — no state_transition to ready appears before the user answers
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→working on the SAME rollout session_id when the user sends the answer (waiting→working recovery)
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→ready on the SAME rollout session_id when the second turn ends with a plain non-question reply (working→ready)
events.jsonl contains a process_exited on the rollout session at REPL teardown (single live session across both turns, not per-turn exits)

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

## Scenario

The canonical primitive is an AskUserQuestion-style **structured blocking-question tool**: the agent pauses mid-turn by invoking a dedicated question primitive that puts the session into a distinct, daemon-observable `waiting-for-user` gate, and stays there until the user replies. The scenario locks down the entry into `waiting` via that structured primitive (not via free-text prose).

## agent_supports: no

opencode has **no AskUserQuestion-equivalent structured blocking-question primitive that surfaces a daemon-observable waiting state via the headless path**. The opencode adapter parses opencode's local SQLite `part` rows, and the only part types it recognises are `step-start`, `step-finish`, `text`, and `tool` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/parser.go`, `ParseLine` switch). The `step-finish` finish reasons it maps are `stop`, `tool-calls`, `interrupted`, `length`, `error`, and `content-filter` — none of which represents a structured ask-the-user gate. There is no `question` / `ask_user` / `elicit` part type, and no finish reason that means "blocked on a user answer." opencode's TUI may prompt the user interactively (e.g. permission hooks), but that is not a model-invoked structured question tool that the headless/store path exposes as a distinct waiting primitive.

This is the structured-tool reading of the scenario, and it is why the fresh column reassessment rated `agent_supports: no` (no AskUserQuestion equivalent matched): the dedicated blocking-question primitive the scenario describes does not exist in opencode. Because the primitive itself is absent at the agent layer, `daemon_capability` and `driver_capability` are `n/a` — there is nothing to observe or drive, not merely something irrlicht can't see yet.

## Why this is a STOP / applicable_false cell

With `agent_supports: no` the cell is definitionally inapplicable for opencode: there is no structured blocking-question turn to elicit, so no spec and no recording. Per the implement-stage #476 routing (`agent_supports ∈ {no}` → STOP), the cell is frozen at `by_adapter.opencode.applicable: false` with a scope note, and the assessment + recipe are committed without a recording.

## Carve-out (the trailing-? text path is a DIFFERENT cell)

Do not confuse this STRUCTURED-tool verdict with the adapter-agnostic trailing-`?` TEXT-classifier path. The opencode metrics reader DOES populate `metrics.LastAssistantText` from `text` parts (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/metrics.go` lines ~227-246), and irrlicht's agent-agnostic `session.IsWaitingForUserInput()` (decision 2a in `state_classifier.go`) would fire on a trailing `?` there — exactly the path the aider × user-blocking-question cell records (`partial`/`yes`). But that text path is NOT the AskUserQuestion-style structured blocking primitive this scenario's `agent_supports` axis is judging; the primitive is absent regardless. This assessment scopes only the structured-tool primitive, per the dispatcher's frozen column reassessment.

## What would flip this

`agent_supports` would move off `no` only if upstream opencode shipped a dedicated structured ask-the-user / elicit tool that the SQLite `part` schema records as its own part type or finish reason, which the adapter could then map to a distinct waiting gate. No such primitive exists in opencode today.
caveats: scope: this verdict is for the AskUserQuestion-style STRUCTURED blocking-question primitive only. The adapter-agnostic trailing-? TEXT-classifier waiting path (session.IsWaitingForUserInput on LastAssistantText) is a separate concern and is NOT what this cell's agent_supports axis judges — opencode's metrics reader does populate LastAssistantText, but that path does not constitute a structured ask-user tool.

opencode's TUI permission hooks / interactive prompts are user-facing UI gates, not a model-invoked structured question tool surfaced through the headless/store path; they do not make agent_supports anything other than no for this primitive.
This is the structured-tool reading frozen by the dispatcher's column reassessment; a future opencode release adding a dedicated ask/elicit part type or finish reason would warrant re-assessment.

sources.note: ParseLine switch handles only step-start / step-finish / text / tool part types; step-finish reasons are stop/tool-calls/interrupted/length/error/content-filter — no question/ask-user/elicit part type or blocking finish reason.
ComputeMetrics sets metrics.LastAssistantText from text parts (lines ~227-246) — feeds the agent-agnostic trailing-? text path, which is a DIFFERENT cell, not a structured AskUserQuestion primitive.

IsWaitingForUserInput / ExtractQuestionSnippet — agent-agnostic waiting detection on LastAssistantText (trailing ?), not a structured question tool; cited to delimit the carve-out, not to support a yes verdict.
Feature set is local TUI + tool_calls + permission_hooks + subagents etc.; no dedicated structured ask-user / question-tool primitive key.
Contrast: aider records the trailing-? TEXT path as partial/yes. That is the text-classifier cell, distinct from this STRUCTURED-tool agent_supports:no verdict for opencode.

recipe.notes: opencode has no AskUserQuestion-equivalent structured blocking-question primitive that surfaces a daemon-observable waiting state via the headless path; the SQLite part schema records only step-start/step-finish/text/tool parts (no question/ask-user/elicit part type or blocking finish reason). agent_supports:no, daemon_capability:n/a. The adapter-agnostic trailing-? TEXT-classifier waiting path is a separate cell, not this structured-tool primitive.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

## Scenario

The `user-blocking-question` primitive is `tool_calls` + AskUserQuestion (or adapter equivalent): the agent pauses **mid-turn** to ask the user a structured question via a dedicated question tool, the dashboard enters `waiting`, and stays there until the user replies — producing the user-observable arc `ready → working → waiting → working → ready` with the `waiting` episode visible (no skip-over).

This is distinct from `tool-gate-permission-prompt` (permission, not a question), `user-blocking-plan-mode-approval` (pause for plan review), and `mid-turn-message-queued` (USER sends unsolicited input; the agent does not request it).

## Reasoning — agent_supports: no

Stock pi (v0.70.0) ships **no dedicated question / elicitation tool**. The built-in tool registry is closed and explicit:

```
allToolNames = new Set(["read", "bash", "edit", "write", "grep", "find", "ls"]);
```

(`dist/core/tools/index.js`). The `dist/core/tools/` directory contains exactly those tools (plus helpers: edit-diff, truncate, path-utils, render-utils, file-mutation-queue, tool-definition-wrapper). There is no `ask`, `question`, `confirm`, `input`, or `elicit` tool.

The Step 2.5 binary guard backs this up. pi is a Node bundle; the real code lives under `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/` (the `cli.js` `which pi` resolves to is a 530-byte launcher). A recursive grep across the whole dist for `askuserquestion|ask_user|askuser|user_question|userquestion|elicit|requestuserinput|request_user_input|prompt_user|promptuser` returned **zero matches**, and a grep of the tool dir for any tool registered with a `name: "ask|question|confirm|input|elicit"` was also empty. A grep of the session writers (`agent-session.js`, `session-manager.js`) for `waiting|waitingForUser|awaiting|blockOnUser` found nothing — pi has no notion of a turn that blocks awaiting structured user input.

The agent's tools all **auto-execute** within a continuous working turn (`permission_hooks: false`, `subagents: false` in capabilities.json); there is no primitive that suspends the turn to solicit a user answer. An extension could add one, but that is not stock pi.

## Reasoning — daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

Because the agent cannot produce the `working → waiting → working` arc, there is nothing to observe and `irrlicht_observes` is `n/a` rather than `no`. Even if it could, the transport confirms there is no path: the pi parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go`) maps only `session` / `model_change` / `thinking_level_change` / `branch_summary` / `custom` / `compaction` and `message` lines (roles `assistant`, `user`, `toolResult`, `bashExecution`). It emits `turn_done` only on `message.stopReason == "stop"`, `assistant` mid-turn otherwise, plus `user_message` / `tool_result`. There is **no waiting-state event kind** and no transcript signal a question tool would write that the parser recognises — so no `waiting` episode could be synthesized for pi even in principle.

## Conclusion

Verdict ratifies the column reassessment (`no` / `n/a`) and the existing coverage matrix (`no` / `n/a`). No matrix drift. The pipeline is frozen at the agent layer; what would unblock it is a future pi release shipping a dedicated AskUserQuestion-style blocking tool (re-run the keyword guard against the new binary) AND a pi-parser change to map that tool to a waiting transition.
caveats: Frozen at the agent layer: a third-party pi extension could register a question tool, but that is not stock pi and is out of scope for the matrix.
Even if pi gained such a tool, the pi parser has no waiting-state mapping today — observability would require an adapter change as well, not just an agent change.

sources.note: ParseLine handles only session/model_change/thinking_level_change/branch_summary/custom/compaction/message (assistant/user/toolResult/bashExecution); no waiting-state event kind, no question-tool mapping.
Step 2.5 guard: allToolNames = {read, bash, edit, write, grep, find, ls} — closed registry, no ask/question/confirm/input/elicit tool. pi v0.70.0.

Step 2.5 binary scan: grep for askuserquestion|ask_user|user_question|elicit|request_user_input|prompt_user across the whole dist returned zero matches; session writers have no waiting/awaiting/blockOnUser. Empty result cited per the guard.
permission_hooks: false, subagents: false — auto-executing tools, no mechanism to suspend a turn awaiting structured user input.

preconditions: pi CLI on PATH and authenticated (pi runs against its configured model out-of-band)
tmux available — the interactive driver runs pi in a tmux REPL so the session stays alive past turn end and the waiting episode is observable
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: The interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) launches pi in a fresh per-run cwd inside a tmux session, waits for the REPL prompt, then sends the single prompt
pi writes one JSONL rollout file per session under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/ on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy); the driver resolves that file and extracts the UUID from line 1

Unlike the headless `pi --print` path (single-shot, the process exits at turn end so the waiting episode is lost to a process_exited race — see the basic-turn recipe), the interactive driver keeps the rollout session ALIVE after the turn; the REPL is torn down only when the script ends, so the daemon can observe the post-turn waiting state dwell

No reply is sent to the question — after wait_turn + a trailing sleep (the session dwells in waiting) the driver kills the tmux session once, so pi exits and the daemon emits a single process_exited for the rollout session while it is still waiting

script.text: I want to add a feature to my project but I'm not sure which file it should go in. Ask me exactly ONE short clarifying question to scope it, ending in a question mark, and then stop and wait for my answer. Output only that one question and nothing else.

verify: events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the rollout session born ready within ~2s of REPL launch (a transient proc-<PID> pre-session may also appear; the turn arc and pid_discovered land on the UUID-keyed rollout session)
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the rollout session_id
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→working on the rollout session_id when the prompt is sent
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→waiting on the SAME rollout session_id when the turn ends (the reply ends in a question mark; the classifier takes the trailing-? path rather than →ready)
the rollout session's final live state is waiting and it persists ≥4s — no state_transition to ready appears before the REPL is torn down
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited on the rollout session at REPL teardown while the session is still waiting (one long-lived session, not a per-turn exit)

_prior_prompt_mode.note: Superseded by the script-mode recipe above. The prior headless prompt-mode recipe (`pi --print -p`) could not observe the waiting episode: in print-mode pi answers the question and the process exits immediately at turn end, so a process_exited race overwrites the working→waiting transition before it can dwell. Script mode keeps the REPL alive so the waiting state is observable.
_prior_prompt_mode.prompt: Reply with exactly one short clarifying question, ending in a question mark. No other text.
